intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry identifies P1 (Yellow Pot), P2/P4 (Black Pot), and P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit). Visual audit confirms P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (Yellow Pot) appears to be empty or contains only substrate. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows a single, thin, possibly etiolated stem/sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears empty.
inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Degraded: Only a single, thin, pale sprout remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Stable: Pothos foliage and rabbit anchor present.

plant_audit.P2/P4: Single sprout, low vigor, signs of etiolation.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos remains the most robust specimen; rabbit anchor is stable.

vision_report.biome_observations: Soil surface in all pots appears dark and potentially saturated. No fungal blooms or surface moss detected. Desk surface is clear of debris.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, there is no significant growth or recovery. The state is stagnant. Image T-2 was a total black-out (sensor failure or lighting error), which has been corrected in subsequent frames.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Stasis/Decline'. The lack of light (North window only) is likely causing the etiolation in P2/P4. P1 is effectively a failed culture.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-botanical feature. No new interventions or unexpected weeds identified.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is struggling. The Pothos (P3) is the only plant maintaining structural integrity. The yellow pot (P1) is currently devoid of life, suggesting a systemic loss of the String of Nickels. The Mexican Mint/Silver Guest (P2/P4) is severely etiolated, indicating insufficient light levels for its metabolic needs. The environment is stable but suboptimal for growth.
